annotations.label: Split-complex number

anchor.snippet: A split-complex number is an ordered pair of real numbers, written in the form

annotations.note: No split-complex number type exists in Mathlib (grep for split-complex returns nothing).

annotations.label: Addition and multiplication

anchor.snippet: Addition and multiplication of split-complex numbers are defined by

annotations.note: The split-complex ring operations are not defined since the type itself is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiplication is commutative, associative, distributive

anchor.snippet: This multiplication is commutative , associative and distributes over addition.

annotations.note: Mathlib's CommRing class abstracts these axioms generically, but they are not instantiated for split-complex numbers.

annotations.label: Split-complex conjugate

anchor.snippet: then the conjugate of z is defined as

annotations.note: Mathlib's StarRing/star provides the general conjugation framework, but the split-complex conjugate map is not defined.

annotations.label: Conjugate is an involution

anchor.snippet: The conjugate is an involution which satisfies similar properties to the complex conjugate

annotations.note: The general involution law star_star holds for any InvolutiveStar, but is not specialized to a split-complex conjugate.

annotations.label: Squared modulus

anchor.snippet: is given by the isotropic quadratic form

annotations.note: Mathlib has a general quadratic-form framework, but the isotropic form a²−b² for split-complex modulus is not a named decl.

annotations.label: Composition algebra property of modulus

anchor.snippet: It has the composition algebra property:

annotations.note: Mathlib has no composition-algebra structure nor a multiplicativity lemma for the split-complex norm.

annotations.label: Modulus has signature (1,-1)

anchor.snippet: However, this quadratic form is not positive-definite but rather has signature

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a developed signature/Sylvester's-law theory for quadratic forms to express signature (1,−1).

annotations.label: Associated bilinear form

anchor.snippet: The associated bilinear form is given by

annotations.note: Mathlib's polarBilin gives the bilinear form associated to a quadratic form generically, but not for the split-complex modulus.

annotations.label: Invertibility and multiplicative inverse

anchor.snippet: A split-complex number is invertible if and only if its modulus is nonzero

annotations.note: The split-complex invertibility criterion and inverse formula z⁻¹ = z̄/N(z) are not present (no such type).

annotations.label: Two nontrivial null idempotents

anchor.snippet: There are two nontrivial idempotent elements given by

annotations.note: The specific idempotents (1±j)/2 of the split-complex ring are not formalized.

annotations.label: Representation in the null basis

anchor.snippet: The split-complex number z can be written in the null basis as

annotations.note: The null/diagonal basis representation of a split-complex number is not formalized.

annotations.label: Arithmetic in the diagonal basis

anchor.snippet: split-complex addition is given by

annotations.note: Componentwise add/multiply is exactly the product ring structure on R × S, but it is not connected to split-complex numbers.

annotations.label: Conjugate and modulus in diagonal basis

anchor.snippet: The split-complex conjugate in the diagonal basis is given by

annotations.note: Component-swap conjugation and product modulus in the diagonal basis are not formalized.

anchors.snippet: The split-complex conjugate in the diagonal basis is given by

annotations.label: Ring isomorphism to direct sum of reals

anchor.snippet: the split-complex numbers are ring-isomorphic to the direct sum

annotations.note: The target ring ℝ × ℝ and the RingEquiv framework exist, but the isomorphism D ≅ ℝ ⊕ ℝ is not stated since D is undefined.

annotations.label: Hyperbolas correspond under the mapping

anchor.snippet: so the two parametrized hyperbolas are brought into correspondence with S

annotations.note: This geometric correspondence of hyperbolas under the diagonalizing map is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic versor as squeeze mapping

anchor.snippet: The action of hyperbolic versor

annotations.note: Squeeze mappings / hyperbolic versor actions are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isomorphism is rotation by 45° and dilation by √2

anchor.snippet: The isomorphism, as a planar mapping, consists of a counter-clockwise rotation by 45°

annotations.note: The geometric description of the diagonalizing map as rotation+dilation is not formalized.

annotations.label: (1+1)-dimensional Minkowski space

anchor.snippet: is called (1 + 1) -dimensional Minkowski space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Minkowski space; only positive-definite/general bilinear forms exist.

annotations.label: Level set is a hyperbola

anchor.snippet: is a hyperbola for every nonzero a in

annotations.note: Hyperbolas as level sets of the modulus are not formalized (no conic-section theory for this).

annotations.label: Unit hyperbola

anchor.snippet: The case a = 1 is called the unit hyperbola

annotations.note: There is no unit-hyperbola definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugate hyperbola

anchor.snippet: The conjugate hyperbola is given by

annotations.note: The conjugate hyperbola is not formalized.

annotations.label: Null cone asymptotes

anchor.snippet: The hyperbola and conjugate hyperbola are separated by two diagonal asymptotes which form the set of null elements

annotations.note: The null cone / asymptote set of split-complex numbers is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic-orthogonality

anchor.snippet: Split-complex numbers z and w are said to be hyperbolic-orthogonal

annotations.note: Hyperbolic orthogonality is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Split-complex Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: The analogue of Euler's formula for the split-complex numbers is

annotations.note: The split-complex exponential exp(jθ)=cosh θ + j sinh θ is not formalized (no split-complex exp), though Real.cosh/sinh exist.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic versor has norm 1

anchor.snippet: the split-complex number λ = exp( jθ ) has norm 1 and lies on the right branch of the unit hyperbola

annotations.note: That exp(jθ) has unit modulus on the hyperbola is not formalized.

annotations.label: Multiplication by λ is a hyperbolic rotation

anchor.snippet: multiplying any split-complex number z by λ preserves the modulus of z and represents a hyperbolic rotation

annotations.note: Hyperbolic rotation by a versor preserving the modulus is not formalized.

annotations.label: Generalized orthogonal group O(1,1)

anchor.snippet: forms a group called the generalized orthogonal group

annotations.note: Mathlib defines only the positive-definite orthogonal group (AAᵀ=1), not the indefinite generalized orthogonal group O(1,1).

annotations.label: Exponential map is a group isomorphism

anchor.snippet: is a group isomorphism since the usual exponential formula applies

annotations.note: The exp isomorphism (ℝ,+) ≅ SO⁺(1,1) for split-complex versors is not formalized.

annotations.label: Polar decomposition off the diagonals

anchor.snippet: then z has a polar decomposition

annotations.note: Polar decomposition z = ρ·exp(jθ) for split-complex numbers is not formalized.

annotations.label: Quotient of polynomial ring presentation

anchor.snippet: the split-complex numbers can be described as the quotient of a polynomial ring

annotations.note: AdjoinRoot gives the general R[X]/(f) construction, but the specific quotient ℝ[X]/(X²−1) is not built/named.

annotations.label: Commutative algebra over the reals

anchor.snippet: the split-complex numbers form a commutative algebra over the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib's Algebra class captures commutative ℝ-algebras generically, but no split-complex instance exists.

annotations.label: Not a field; null elements not invertible

anchor.snippet: The algebra is not a field since the null elements are not invertible.

annotations.note: The claim that split-complex numbers fail to be a field due to non-invertible null elements is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nonzero null elements are zero divisors

anchor.snippet: All of the nonzero null elements are zero divisors.

annotations.note: Mathlib has a general zero-divisor notion, but the split-complex null elements being zero divisors is not stated.

annotations.label: Topological ring

anchor.snippet: the split-complex numbers form a topological ring

annotations.note: Mathlib's IsTopologicalRing class is the general framework, but it is not instantiated for split-complex numbers.

annotations.label: Composition algebra

anchor.snippet: The algebra of split-complex numbers forms a composition algebra since

annotations.note: Mathlib has no composition-algebra structure.

annotations.label: Isomorphic to group ring of C2

anchor.snippet: the ring of split-complex numbers is isomorphic to the group ring

annotations.note: MonoidAlgebra provides the group ring k[G], but the isomorphism D ≅ ℝ[C₂] is not stated (no split-complex type).

annotations.label: Identity component elements have four square roots

anchor.snippet: Elements of the identity component in the group of units in D have four square roots

annotations.note: The four-square-roots property of identity-component units in D is not formalized.

annotations.label: Idempotents are their own square roots

anchor.snippet: are their own square roots

annotations.note: The statement that the split-complex idempotents are their own square roots is not formalized.

annotations.label: Matrix representation by I and J

anchor.snippet: The real unit 1 and hyperbolic unit j can be represented by any pair of matrices

annotations.note: The matrix representation of the split-complex units is not formalized.

annotations.label: Standard 2×2 matrix representation

anchor.snippet: The most common choice is to represent 1 and j by the 2 × 2 identity matrix

annotations.note: The standard 2×2 matrix model of split-complex numbers is not formalized.

annotations.label: General trace-zero det -1 matrix; determinant equals modulus

anchor.snippet: any real-valued 2 × 2 matrix with a trace of zero and a determinant of negative one squares to

annotations.note: The trace-zero/det−1 characterization and det = modulus correspondence are not formalized (general Matrix.det/trace exist but not this statement).

annotations.label: Dirac matrix representation

anchor.snippet: Larger matrices could also be used

annotations.note: Larger (Dirac-matrix) representations of split-complex numbers are not formalized.

annotations.label: Future quadrant polar decomposition

anchor.snippet: The future corresponds to the quadrant of events

annotations.note: The future-quadrant polar decomposition interpretation is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: The future corresponds to the quadrant of events

annotations.label: Additivity of rapidities

anchor.snippet: expressing products on the unit hyperbola illustrates the additivity of rapidities for collinear velocities

annotations.note: Additivity of rapidities via products on the unit hyperbola is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: expressing products on the unit hyperbola illustrates the additivity of rapidities for collinear velocities

annotations.label: Canonical events are hyperbolic-orthogonal

anchor.snippet: are hyperbolic orthogonal and lie on the axes of a frame of reference

annotations.note: The hyperbolic-orthogonality of canonical events on reference-frame axes is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nine-point hyperbola

anchor.snippet: used the split-complex geometric arithmetic to establish the nine-point hyperbola

annotations.note: The nine-point hyperbola construction is not formalized in Mathlib.
